about.description: I'm Adriel, product designer and trainer. My mission: simplify SaaS creation, inspire and guide those who want to bring their ideas to life.
about.keywords: about Adriel Zimbril, SaaS Product Designer, SaaS trainer, SaaS product design journey, ShiroSaaS method

craft-section.description: Putting the user at the center of every decision, guiding and teaching creators to turn every idea into unforgettable experiences.
craft-section.title: Turning ideas into products that matter.

header-section.description: Since my early days in design, I’ve learned that the most successful product isn’t the one you notice first, but the one you understand, test, and evolve with intention.
header-section.description-secondary:
Sic itur ad astra — this is how you reach the stars 🫰🏻✨.

Since I was a kid, I’ve always been fascinated by technology and invention. Iron Man made me believe in the possible: I remember my eyes lighting up at his armor and the inspiration I felt with every episode. I spent hours imagining, tinkering, and dreaming of building my own robots.

Letting this passion run free, I discovered design, and over time, I realized my dream wasn’t just to build machines: I wanted to create experiences that touch people, solve real problems, and make you want to use them. Design became my playground, my lab to imagine, build, and turn raw ideas into real products and craft experiences that matter.

Today, I turn ideas into real experiences, helping founders, teams, and creators clarify their problems, imagine solutions, and launch quickly. My approach combines thoughtful design, agile strategy, and continuous learning so every project has real impact.

I love questioning what exists, exploring what works and what doesn’t, and building with intention. I prefer doing things with a why rather than out of habit.

At the same time, I share methods and resources from my creative experiences so others can learn to design and launch products themselves, with confidence.

For me, design isn’t just a deliverable: it’s a collaborative, creative, and human process ❣️.

content.item1: What works (and what doesn't) in the interfaces I design
content.item2: User feedback that made me change my mind
content.item3: My mistakes and how I try to avoid them
content.subtitle: My thoughts on:
content.title: In this newsletter, I simply share what I learn.

cta.description: A guide on UX mistakes I often see in SaaS. Practical insights I've gathered from my projects.
cta.note: And of course, you can unsubscribe anytime. I only write when I have something useful to share.
cta.title: I've prepared something to get you started.

newsletterSignup.preview: Hi! I wanted to share something close to my heart...

story.intro: A few weeks ago, a user of one of my apps wrote to me saying it took them a while to undo an action. I realized it was because of a design choice I liked... but it made the experience harder.
story.philosophy: What really interests me is understanding how people use what we create. Why do they drop off at certain points, why do they come back, what really helps them in their daily lives.
story.presentation: My name is Adriel, and for 5 years I've been designing interfaces for SaaS. I've noticed something: we often make things more complicated, while users just want simplicity.

newsletterSignup.subject: Hello, it's Adriel !
newsletterSignup.tagline: Problem Solver & Your SaaS Product Designer 🦄

why.motivation: I want to share those little discoveries, those moments when you think, "Oh, I wouldn't have thought of that." No pretension, just shared curiosity ❤️.
why.reason: Because I love understanding how things work. And in design, there's often a gap between what we think will work and what actually does.
why.title: Why do I really write?

productDelivery.message1: I'm glad you're part of this journey. I hope this resource helps you go further, faster.
productDelivery.message2: Your feedback really helps me improve my content for others. And if you have any questions along the way, don't hesitate to ask!

code.feedback: When you've had a chance to use it, I'd love to know what you think. Is it useful for your current projects? Is there anything you're still wondering about?
code.tip: 🚀 Tip: Try to apply what you learn right away.

course.feedback: After going through a few modules, I'd love to hear your thoughts. Does the content meet your expectations? Are there topics you'd like to dive deeper into?
course.tip: 💡 Pro tip: take notes as you go through the modules—it really helps you remember and apply what you learn.

ebook.feedback: When you've had a chance to read it, I'd love to know what you think. Is it useful for your current projects? Is there anything you're still wondering about?
ebook.tip: 📖 Suggestion: Read it in small sections and try to apply what you learn right away.

figma-template.feedback: When you've had a chance to use it, I'd love to know what you think. Is it useful for your current projects? Is there anything you're still wondering about?
figma-template.tip: 🚀 Tip: Try to apply what you learn right away.

video.feedback: Once you've watched the masterclass, let me know what you think! Did it answer your questions? What stood out the most?
video.tip: 🎯 Tip: Watch it in a quiet place with a notebook nearby. The best ideas often come while you're listening.

projects.description: A look at the projects I’ve designed and launched, with the process and results. Not just pretty screens: stories of collaboration and success.

goal-research-section.description: From this problem, we draw the project’s compass: the objectives and indicators that will show if we’re on the right track.
goal-research-section.title: Goal?

project-points-research-section.description: Every piece of data and feedback turns into lessons that inform design choices and guide our decisions.
project-points-research-section.title: Insights

project-preview-section.description: Every screen tells a story: how users discover the product, interact with it, and experience it in real life.
project-preview-section.title: Preview

project-research-section.description: It all starts with the problem: who is affected, in what context, and why it deserves our attention.
project-research-section.title: Problem framing

project-result-section.description: The project comes to life and tells its success through adoption, retention, and its impact on users and the business.
project-result-section.title: Results

project-statement-section.description: User needs and behaviors are revealed through interviews, observations, and conversations, showing us their needs, frustrations, and motivations.
project-statement-section.title: User research
